data.title: Archer Aviation (ACHR) Gains U.S. Backing as Analyst Sees 110% Upside
data.description:
The U.S. Department of Transportation has recently picked eight pilot projects for its new eVTOL Integration Pilot Program, about 270 days after launch. The program, built in collaboration with the Federal Aviation Administration, aims to test next-wave air travel in real-world settings across 26 states. Meanwhile, Canaccord's four-star analyst Austin Moeller doubled down on ACHR shares with a $13 price target and about 110% upside.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Notably, the plan brings in eight makers, including Archer Aviation (ACHR) and Joby Aviation (JOBY). State groups such as Texas, Florida, and New York will work with these firms to test use cases like city rides and cargo trips. In one case, the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey will run 12 test plans, with some routes tied to Manhattan.

Archer Aviation said it will work with Texas, Florida, and New York to prep for its Midnight craft. The firm aims to start its first flights in the second half of 2026.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade ACHR
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: 'Backfired': A Rare Downside With Buying SPY, Says Top Investor
data.description:
For risk-averse investors seeking to gain exposure to major U.S. firms, the SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) has been a trusty companion throughout the years. The ETF is widely recognized as a premier vehicle directly linked to the S&P 500, a benchmark index that represents 500 of the largest publicly-traded companies on the U.S. equity market.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In general, ETFs provide a solid hedge against volatility, as the combination of multiple assets into a single entity can protect against catastrophic losses if a single company goes bankrupt. That's arguably even less likely to occur with SPY, as large-cap companies tend to be more stable than their smaller peers.

The ETF has roughly $665 billion in assets under management and a beta of 1.00, meaning that its performance tracks with the larger market. Though its expense ratio of 0.09% isn't exactly dirt cheap, SPY provides a practical way to take advantage of strong historic performance of the American business sector.

It's a bet that's played out well over the years, and its 1-year, 3-year, and 10-year returns of 17%, 68%, and 223% speak to its healthy track record. And yet, this year, SPY has been struggling, logging a loss of around 3%.

Top investor James Brumley explains that this sort of thing can happen.

"A very unusual set of circumstances has temporarily caused this usually smart strategy to backfire," notes the 5-star investor, who is among the top 1% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade SPY

Brumley points out that a number of sectors have actually been doing decently over the past few months. He notes that, since the end of November, for instance, the performance of certain sectors (such as Energy, Materials, and Industrials) has actually been rather strong.

And yet, SPY has lagged these results.

"If you're a fan of index investing, you need to know this sort of thing is possible," adds Brumley.

That's because SPY isn't sector-balanced, explains Brumley; it's a cap-weighted fund. The biggest names on the market stem from the technology sector, which has been weighed down by fears of AI capex spending.

Moreover, technology and financials – both of which have lost ground over the past few months – are disproportionately represented in the S&P 500.

Brumley therefore suggests that investors take the possibility of these types of dips into account, "particularly when different sectors reach extreme valuations, or new geopolitical tensions rattle a particular sliver of the market." (To watch James Brumley's track record, click here)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Dow Jones Stalls as Jobless Claims Disappoint
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open lower on Thursday following the release of new jobs data from the Department of Labor.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Have high conviction in QQQ? Leverage your view

For the week ended March 14, initial jobless claims fell by 8,000 to 205,000, below the consensus estimate of 215,000. Continuing jobless claims, which trail initial claims by a week, rose by 10,000 to 1.857 million and were slightly above the expectation of 1.852 million. Initial jobless claims serve as a gauge of layoffs, while continuing jobless claims track the number of people receiving unemployment benefits and reflect ongoing unemployment.

*This is a developing story. Stay tuned for additional updates.*

data.title: Rivian Stock (RIVN) Revs Up 8% as Uber Plans $1.25B Investment, R2 SUVs as Robotaxis
data.description:
Rivian's (RIVN) shares entered high gear early Thursday, jumping about 8% after Uber Technologies (UBER) announced plans to invest up to $1.25 billion into the U.S. electric vehicle maker. The ride-hailing company also intends to deploy about 10,000 of the fully driverless versions of Rivian's newly launched R2 electric SUVs for robotaxi service, starting in 2028.

data.title: Stock Futures Fall as Oil Prices Rise amid Intensifying U.S.-Iran Conflict
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Thursday morning as the U.S.-Iran war intensified, pushing oil prices higher. Meanwhile, the U.S. Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged yesterday but warned of higher inflationary pressure. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.65%, 0.47%, and 0.47%, respectively, at 8:41 a.m. EST on March 19.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, the international oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up 5.56% at $113.23 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) traded higher by 0.73% at $96.97 per barrel. The surge in international oil was sparked by Iran's attack on a major liquefied natural gas (LNG) export facility in Qatar. Also, Israel attacked Iran's South Pars gas field, to which Iran retaliated by hitting Qatari energy facilities.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower after Fed Chair Jerome Powell cautioned that surging oil prices could stoke inflation fears. The Dow Jones fell to a fresh 2026 low, tumbling 1.63%, while the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite declined 1.36% and 1.46%, respectively.

Turning to trending stocks, Micron Technology (MU) shares fell more than 5% in Thursday's pre-market trading as concerns about the chip company's high capital spending overshadowed better-than-expected Fiscal Q2 sales and earnings. Alibaba (BABA) stock fell about 5% after the Chinese e-commerce giant reported disappointing fiscal third-quarter results.

In contrast, Five Below (FIVE) stock rose more than 6% after the discount retailer reported better-than-expected Q4 results and issued strong guidance. Also, shares of Align Technology (ALGN) soared more than 6% on news that Elliott Investment Management built a significant stake in the company.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: TSMC Stock (TSM) Wilts as Iranian Attacks Batter Helium Supply and Threaten Chip Production
data.description:
Major semiconductor stock TSMC (TSM) was lower today on fears that production will soon be hit by a lack of helium because of the Iran war. AI stock Nvidia (NVDA) and EV maker Tesla (TSLA) could also soon be in the firing line because of the rapid hike in helium prices.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The recent attacks on energy facilities in the Gulf States by Iran following the outbreak of war with the U.S. and Israel have seen gas and oil prices spike higher.

Helium is Pivotal in Semiconductor Production

But the gas supply disruption has also tightened the supply of helium, which is a natural gas byproduct and pivotal in semiconductor manufacturing.

Qatar, which suffered a pounding from Iranian missiles last night, is the world's second-largest helium producer, supplying a third of global output.

Putting on a science hat, helium is essential in creating the right conditions for semiconductor manufacturing. That includes creating a stable vacuum environment during the lithography process, as well as cooling semiconductor materials and reducing thermal stress that could damage the integrity and quality of the chips. It is also used for leak detection in sub-5-nanometer chip fabrication. In short, advanced processors can't be made without it.

The closure of the Ras Laffan facility, the world's largest LNG plant and one of the Iranian targets, since the beginning of March has taken roughly 5.2 million cubic meters of helium off the market each month.

Trade NVDA with leverage

Helium Prices No Laughing Matter

As such, helium prices are up 13% over the last five days. If disruptions last 60 to 90 days, experts say prices could surge by another 25-50%, potentially exceeding $2,000 per thousand cubic feet, or more than four times pre-war levels.

There are alternative suppliers such as the U.S., Algeria, Russia and Poland, but there is a risk without more that the world's semiconductor fabs may have to shut down production.

TSMC is the most exposed to the shortage of helium. The company has said operations remain normal for now and that inventories and supply are manageable.

"Even if Qatar's facilities restart, the helium shortage may not end quickly. Qatar's energy minister says normal deliveries could take weeks to months to resume after the conflict ends. The lag would likely extend a "catch-up" period for shipping schedules and contract allocations, keeping spot prices elevated and increasing the likelihood of tighter supply discipline by industrial gas distributors," said ratings agency Fitch.

Susannah Streeter, Chief Investment Strategist at Wealth Club, said: "There are concerns about helium supplies being stranded, a key component in semiconductor manufacturing, which could lead to delays in producing electronic goods and even cars."

We have rounded up the best chip stocks to buy now using our TipRanks comparison tool. As can be seen below, Broadcom (AVGO) has the best upside of 48.03%.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: LUNR Earnings: Intuitive Machines Stock Sinks After Its Q4 Revenue Missed Wall Street Estimate
data.description:
Intuitive Machines (LUNR) stock fell on Thursday following the release of the space manufacturing and supply company's Q4 2025 earnings report. This report started with revenue of $44.79 million, which was well below the $53.71 million that Wall Street expected. Investors will also note that the company's revenue fell 18.05% year-over-year from $54.66 million.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Intuitive Machines didn't provide earnings per share data in its latest earnings report. However, the company did report a net loss of $59.66 million. That's a major improvement over the net loss of $165.15 million reported in the fourth quarter of 2024. The company attributed its shrinking net loss to the completion of its second lunar mission, its expansion into national security space programs, its acquisition of KinetX Aerospace, and other factors.

Intuitive Machines stock was down 3.87% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 4.18% fall yesterday. Even so, the stock has rallied 11.58% year-to-date and 154.71% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 3/19/26 – Stock Futures Slump as Iran War Intensifies, Fed Warns of Inflation
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell early Thursday as the Israel-Iran war intensified, pushing oil prices higher. The U.S. Federal Reserve kept the interest rate unchanged yesterday but warned of higher inflationary pressure. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 Index (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.56%, 0.42%, and 0.38%, respectively, at 5:36 a.m. EST on March 19.

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the time of writing, the global oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) was up 3.29% at $115.48 a barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) traded near $96.81 per barrel.

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed lower after Fed Chair Jerome Powell cautioned that surging oil prices could stoke inflation fears. The Dow Jones fell to a fresh 2026 low, tumbling 1.63%, while the S&P 500 shed 1.36% and the Nasdaq dropped 1.46%.

After markets closed, Micron Technology (MU) shares fell more than 4% despite beating fiscal Q2 sales and earnings expectations. The chipmaker's revenues nearly tripled, fueled by a memory chip supply shortage, and guided above consensus for Q3. However, its increased capex guidance left investors worried about returns.

Looking ahead to economic reports, data on building permits, new home sales, weekly jobless claims, and Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index are due today.

Trade QQQ with leverage

On the earnings front, notable companies reporting today include Alibaba (BABA), FedEx (FDX), and Accenture (ACN).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.28%. Additionally, the Spot Gold U.S. dollar price traded around $4,720 per ounce on Thursday.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened lower amid the escalating war. Chip companies are already facing shortages as the war has halted freight routes.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Lower

Asia-Pacific markets traded lower on March 19 as global investors digested the impact of the Israel-Iran war.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index fell 2.02%. In China, the Shanghai Composite dropped 1.39%, while the Shenzhen Component declined 2.36%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei plunged 3.38%, with the Topix losing 2.91%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Why Is China Natural Resources Stock (CHNR) Soaring Today?
data.description:
China Natural Resources (CHNR) stock rocketed higher on Thursday after the Chinese metal mining company announced a non-binding Letter of Intent (LOI) with Feishang Group Limited. This agreement will have China Natural Resources acquire a 100% stake in a Feishang Group Limited subsidiary that will own 59.79% of the equity interest of HooRii Technology. Investors will also note that Feishang Group Limited is the principal shareholder of China Natural Resources.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

China Natural Resources' plans to take a stake in HooRii Technology will expand its influence beyond mining and into AI. HooRii Technology is a Chinese physical AI company focused on combining Internet of Things (IoT) and AI. China Natural Resources noted that it plans to strengthen its mining operations alongside its shift to AI, allowing it to have a dual focus.

The finer details of the acquisition agreement are still being worked out. However, the LOI includes a potential purchase price of $37 million to $40 million for the 100% stake in the Feishang Group Limited subsidiary. China Natural Resources intends to pay this via a mix of cash and stock issuance. The company didn't provide a timeframe for when the deal would be completed.

China Natural Resources Stock Rallies Today

China Natural Resources stock was up 66.06% in pre-market trading on Thursday, following a 1.49% rally yesterday. The shares have fallen 8.33% year-to-date and 28.89% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of CHNR stock, as more than 16.6 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 42,000 shares.
data.author: William White
